task: Turn standalone XBRL Facts into causal financial evidence analysis
description: Redesign the standalone XBRL Canvas container around classified SEC evidence, actionable derived metrics, evidence-weighted scores, and filing-to-filing change rather than a flat raw-facts table.
progress: Upgraded the analysis authority to an explicit v2 closed-form contract. Every profitability, growth, cash-quality, balance-sheet, and capital-discipline component now exposes its raw value, unit, normalization bounds and direction, component and category weights, normalized score, coverage, effective weight, and contribution points. The composite excludes withheld low-coverage categories and remains causal. Reported evidence now retains every comparable causal observation filed from January 2019 through the selected clock, period-over-period change, a defensible semantic direction, freshness, and filing audit fields. Rebuilt the container around a large overall decision, selectable overall or category gradient trajectories, readable weighted category cards, an inspectable calculation panel, larger derived metrics, reported-evidence cards with per-field gradient histories, and expandable source details. The time-proportional chart now uses an external Y-axis gutter, seven date ticks, purple gradient analytical context, and green or red only for favorable or unfavorable evidence. The comprehensive guide and architecture document explain the objective, exact formulas, every category and range, coverage rules, history, colors, causality, audit trail, and limitations.
final_result_or_next_dependency: Sixteen focused ticker-facts backend tests pass, including the v2 score contract, 2019 query boundary, revision-safe historical selection, coverage withholding, causal filing states, comparable reported history, and semantic change tone. The frontend TypeScript and Vite production build passes. Browser validation against a representative 2019-to-2026 causal fixture confirmed Jan 2019 and six additional time ticks, an external non-overlapping Y-axis, purple composite line and gradient, semantic green histories, and zero horizontal overflow at normal and compact widths. Live ClickHouse was unavailable, so prior live AAPL findings are not reused as validation for this follow-up.
program_contribution: Turns canonical SEC XBRL history into an explainable, point-in-time financial quality system whose scores are reproducible by strategies and whose conclusions remain readable and auditable in Canvas.

task: Compare deterministic, v1, and v2 news-reaction P&L
description: Evaluate the deterministic language model, embedding classifier v1, and regression v2 on one identical 2026 article-horizon ledger and report separate one-share long, short, flat, and total gross P&L by horizon.
progress: Added a reusable comparison evaluator and workstation launcher. Exact identity includes canonical news ID, ticker, publication timestamp, and horizon. V1 uses class-head argmax, v2 uses the 0.25 training-scale flat band, and deterministic v2.1 uses persisted classes while explicitly counting unavailable predictions as flat. JSON and CSV artifacts retain long and short counts and P&L independently plus totals across horizons.
final_result_or_next_dependency: Seventeen focused tests and Python compilation pass. The live comparison matched all 236,938 v2 ledger rows; deterministic predictions existed for 217,571 rows and 19,367 unavailable predictions were explicitly flat. Across independently treated horizons, deterministic gross one-share P&L was plus 1,086.89 dollars, v1 was plus 4,346.17 dollars, and v2 was minus 1,176.02 dollars. These are descriptive signal ledgers before costs, overlap, capital constraints, and execution effects.
program_contribution: Creates a common evidence base for comparing model direction quality without changing labels, entry prices, horizons, or validation membership between methods.

progress: Added one canonical performance_snapshot contract to live, replay, and backtest state. It defines New-York-session net P&L as realized flat-to-flat episodes closed today plus current unrealized P&L, counts nonzero positions, retains realized and unrealized components, and publishes broker available funds with a total-cash fallback and explicit basis. Canvas requests a compact canonical snapshot with a version-tolerant normalization path, uses completion-based single-flight refreshes, pauses while hidden, and retains the last trustworthy account-matched snapshot across reloads with an explicit stale state until refresh. Independent IBKR reads execute through bounded concurrency. The Performance lead-in sizes to its content, while five adjacent equal compact metric cells use larger primary numbers; the remaining toolbar track stays empty and expands before Canvas actions.
final_result_or_next_dependency: Fifteen canonical trading tests pass, including a concurrency regression test; Python compilation and the frontend TypeScript/Vite production build pass. Exact browser timing reproduced 18.46 seconds of empty metrics before the fix. A current-code endpoint returned the five canonical values in 2.84 seconds, and browser reload review immediately retained +$50 Net P&L, one open position, +$50 unrealized, $0 realized today, and about $715K available cash while marked stale, then refreshed to ready. Browser geometry verified five contiguous 110-pixel metric cells, flexible empty toolbar space, and 14-pixel primary values; the longest exercised value, $714.8K, matched its available text width without clipping. The production build passes and no task-started service remains running.
program_contribution: Makes account risk and performance continuously visible across every Canvas arrangement while keeping one cross-mode calculation authority, eliminating cumulative broker-read latency, surviving reload and deployment boundaries, and leaving room for future global metrics.

task: Add causal QMD structural-pressure likelihood indicator
description: Add one support field and one resistance field around current price, combine them into a conservative directional likelihood, preserve the existing QMD indicators, and expose the result to strategies and Canvas charts.
progress: Added a bounded causal structural-pressure calculation to the canonical GenericStructureEngine. Every active zone on its valid side contributes freshness-decayed strength, confidence, scale reliability, and exponential distance proximity. Overlapping levels are clustered; same-scale duplicates and cross-scale overlap receive independence discounts; each side combines at most twelve nearest clusters. The gateway emits support field, resistance field, signed pressure bias, directional confidence, and a deterministic implied up likelihood that shrinks to 50 percent when evidence is weak or conflicted. Indicator schema v15 and qmd-derived-v15 propagate the same fields through live rows, ClickHouse persistence, historical replay, indicator catalog, and strategy-facing payloads without replacing QMD Generic Structure or any other indicator. Canvas adds an optional QMD Structural Pressure oscillator with semantic support/resistance lines, sign-colored bias, implied likelihood, optional confidence, full legend configuration, and a guide that explicitly distinguishes the score from a calibrated probability. The value-tag follow-up replaces the shared Field series title with distinct Support and Resistance titles so the green and red oscillator-axis labels identify their actual measures.
final_result_or_next_dependency: All 63 shared QMD Gateway tests pass, including three new focused tests for bullish and bearish asymmetry, balanced-field uncertainty, distance decay, and duplicate-cluster discounting. All 14 QMD History tests pass, Rust formatting passes, and the frontend TypeScript/Vite production build passes. Real in-app browser review confirmed the new Price Action catalog entry, independent pane, five configurable legend series, pane threshold controls, and complete guide; the test selection was restored afterward. The value-tag correction passes the frontend production build, and populated browser inspection confirms green Support and red Resistance labels instead of two Field labels; the temporary indicator selection was restored. Existing user-managed services were left untouched.
program_contribution: Provides one efficient and strategy-readable summary of the full causal support/resistance map while retaining exact level overlays for diagnosis and avoiding false certainty from duplicated or conflicting structural evidence.

task: Replace timeframe structure overlays with event-native QMD structure
description: Replace the candle-timeframe liquidity-support and market-structure implementations with one causal, strategy-readable structure authority derived from ordered NBBO and eligible trades, retain crucial history efficiently, and integrate a clearer configurable chart package.
progress: Added a shared deterministic GenericStructureEngine that processes each ordered symbol event exactly once before any timeframe sampling. NBBO midpoint drives adaptive directional-change pivots at micro, tactical, and context price-response scales; eligible trades confirm accepted breaks and build exact trade-volume POC. The engine emits causal pivot and confirmation timestamps, BoS, CHoCH, touch, hold, and break events, active multi-scale support and resistance zones, unified direction, strength, confidence, scale agreement, and signed score. Live and historical bars now sample the same snapshot at aligned bar ends. Confirmed events persist idempotently in qmd_structure_events_v1 and one full versioned engine checkpoint per changed symbol persists in qmd_structure_state_v1 by default, coalesced once per bounded writer flush and independently of optional full bar-indicator persistence. The checkpoint retains adaptive EWMAs, pending directional state, zones, session references, exact eligible-trade volume-at-price, and the last causal event so restart continuation is exact. QMD History performs a symbol-bounded, 90-day, 5,000-event causal warm start and never reads confirmations after the requested window. Removed the active legacy liquidity, candle-structure, and confluence implementations and replaced their Canvas migration, legend, guide, oscillator, zones, reference levels, and settings with one QMD Generic Structure package. Visualization retains current zones plus only bounded strong durable historical zones, limits BoS and CHoCH connectors, keeps full history in the database, uses confidence for line opacity and width and strength for region density, exposes compact right-axis tags, and remains independently configurable by scale, side, event, and reference group. Updated contracts, configuration, architecture, history documentation, and the browser-review assertion to the new package. A presentation follow-up makes Current support and resistance the sole default zone map, renames Decision zones to the opt-in Selected structure zones diagnostic, leaves micro, tactical, and context zones opt-in, and renders swing, break, session, auction, LULD, completed-period, and round-price references as true lines. Their style, width, history, historical labels, label limit, axis tags, and final 0-100 percent opacity now control the actual primitive instead of multiplying a hidden low-opacity base. Current support and resistance price-axis tags now use the same confidence-adjusted semantic color and configured opacity as their chart regions; zero opacity removes the tag as well as the region. A follow-up traced Lightweight Charts 5.2.0 discarding alpha while generating price-axis contrast colors and replaces unsupported RGBA with theme-aware precomposition through the supported axisLabelColor contract, so partial settings such as 30 percent visibly affect the tag.
final_result_or_next_dependency: All 56 shared QMD gateway tests and all 14 QMD History tests pass after Rust formatting. The event-native regression proves aligned 100 ms, 1 second, and 5 second endpoints receive identical structure; focused tests cover causal pivots, out-of-order rejection, one-time accepted breaks, opposing-scale agreement, trade-volume POC, compact visible-state restoration, and a full checkpoint JSON round-trip with exact continuation. The frontend TypeScript and Vite production build passes. A real AAPL 10-second history request returned 1,273 indicator rows with a nonzero signed structure score, confidence, support, resistance, and event state; a warm bounded request completed in 1.781 seconds with QMD History cache hits and no eviction. Strict browser review passed with zero objective issues at Light 100 percent 1440 by 1000 after 30 mixed interaction cycles, Dark 80 percent 1100 by 760, and Graphite 125 percent 1600 by 1000. Every run retained 29 canvases, finite rendering, zero scale recovery, and a readable decluttered structure view. The line-configuration follow-up production build also passes; live-browser checks confirmed the recommended default hierarchy, complete swing and opening-range configuration controls, opaque line rendering behind candles, and clean presentation in Light 125 percent and Dark 80 percent with no console warnings or errors. The axis-tag opacity follow-up also passes the production build and a live Dark 100 percent browser review with populated current zones and no console warnings or errors. Source inspection confirmed the third-party renderer converts label backgrounds to opaque RGB; the corrected build passes after supplying a precomposed axis-label color, and the live chart remains error-free. A latest-event follow-up fixes compact BoS and CHoCH tags that were discarded when a causal pivot and confirmation mapped into the same selected candle: the connector retains its true event endpoints while the tag uses the nearest collision-free lane beside the confirmation candle. The latest-event follow-up also passes the production frontend build; the July 17 AAPL 10-second browser reproduction rendered recent BoS and CHoCH tags, and the strict dark-theme Canvas review reported zero objective layout or blank-page issues. All task-started validation services were stopped.
program_contribution: Establishes one persistent, causal, timeframe-independent market-structure authority that strategies and charts can share while preserving full historical evidence without making the interactive chart render every transient state.

task: Clarify chart level labels and encode liquidity confidence
description: Reduce current-bar obstruction from QMD liquidity and market-structure overlays and make genuine confidence visible in the level treatment without inventing confidence for deterministic structure.
progress: Shortened every current liquidity and market-structure label to its compact tag plus fixed-precision price while preserving the full evidence label as accessible hover text. QMD liquidity strength now controls region density, while genuine support or resistance confidence independently controls border weight, opacity, and semantic color saturation. Market-structure levels retain neutral configured styling because their deterministic contract exposes no confidence field. Updated both indicator guides to explain compact tags, the independent strength and confidence channels, and the distinction between evidence quality and forecast probability. The price-axis follow-up adds persistent native compact tags for current important levels without adding another overlay layer. SUP and RES, premarket H/L, opening-range H/L, and POC enable the tags by default; session, swings, estimated LULD, 52-week, prior-month, and round-price groups expose the same independent legend setting with conservative defaults. Native price-line runtimes are keyed, updated only when price, title, theme color, or visibility changes, and removed when their level or indicator leaves the active selection. The candle-clear annotation follow-up removes the redundant current in-plot detail-label renderer, its legend toggle, collision layout, and unused CSS so current values are read from the native price axis. Historical marker anchors now derive from the source candle's actual high, low, and wick geometry: resistance, swing-high, and bullish-break evidence is placed above; support, swing-low, and bearish-break evidence below; and neutral or edge-constrained evidence to the left. Compact historical tags follow the same directional anchor and are omitted when the reserved outside-candle box cannot fit safely. The line-attached annotation follow-up replaces boxed marker-side tags with borderless semantic text positioned along the evidence span. BoS and CHoCH labels now sit between the confirmed swing and breaking bar, while swing and QMD liquidity labels sit on their historical level segments. Candidate positions are tested from the span midpoint outward and rejected when they intersect candle bodies, wicks, or another line label; recent BoS and CHoCH events remain eligible because they describe a completed relationship rather than a current price level. Current levels continue to use only native right-axis tags. The final marker-removal follow-up deletes the price-zone candle-anchor subsystem, including swing triangles, BoS squares, CHoCH X marks, liquidity hooks, marker-size settings, and their draw routines. Liquidity and market structure now use only evidence lines or regions, inline historical labels, confidence styling where available, and native current price-axis tags; the chart's independent trade-marker facility is unchanged. The price-axis coordinate follow-up disables Lightweight Charts label collision alignment on both right and left scales so indicator tags remain at their true value coordinate during vertical price panning and scaling instead of being stacked into screen-stable slots.
final_result_or_next_dependency: The frontend TypeScript and Vite production build passes. Populated AAPL 100 ms browser review confirmed SUP, RES, OR H, SwH, SwL, and CHoCH compact current tags with fixed price precision and genuine liquidity confidence and strength metadata. Light at 80 percent desktop and Graphite at 125 percent compact reviews reported zero objective issues, with readable semantic emphasis and no fabricated market-structure confidence. The price-axis review confirmed native RES, SUP, OR H, POC, and other enabled tags remain aligned with their right-axis prices beside VWAP in Light and Graphite at 100 and 125 percent scales. The persisted QMD support setting removed and restored the SUP axis tag immediately without disturbing its chart zone or price scale. The deterministic harness aborted its seeded history request, so populated verification used the live in-app chart instead. A populated AAPL 10-second regression in Graphite at 100 percent and Light at 125 percent confirmed there are no current detail-label DOM nodes, native right-axis tags remain intact, resistance and bullish markers sit above candle wicks, support and low markers sit below them, and compact tags preserve clear candle bodies in both dense and compact layouts. The line-attached annotation review used populated AAPL 10-second history in Graphite at 100 percent and Light at 125 percent across 1280 by 720 and 1920 by 1080 viewports. It confirmed BoS and CHoCH text reads within connector spans, SwH and SwL plus SUP and RES text attaches to historical level spans, dense conflicts are omitted, and the browser console remained free of warnings and errors. The final populated AAPL 10-second regression in Graphite at 100 percent and Light at 125 percent confirmed all liquidity and structure candle markers are absent, lines and inline labels remain readable, the marker-size legend control is absent, current right-axis tags remain intact, and the browser console is free of warnings and errors. The price-axis follow-up reproduces the pinned-label behavior on populated AAPL 10-second data, then confirms repeated vertical pan and scale gestures keep price, overlay, structure, and oscillator tags at their actual coordinates in Dark and Light at 100 percent with no browser warnings or errors; the frontend production build passes. The user's Graphite 100 percent appearance was restored, and existing user-managed services were left running and untouched.
program_contribution: Makes dense chart levels faster to scan and communicates evidence quality through redundant visual channels while preserving the semantic boundary between probabilistic liquidity evidence and deterministic price structure.

description: Create a compact single-symbol Canvas container that organizes canonical ticker, issuer, listing, capitalization, share supply, volume, short positioning, IBKR borrow, fundamentals, corporate actions, identifiers, classifications, and source availability without estimating missing facts.
progress: Added one bounded point-in-time backend contract anchored to the canonical US tradable identity and concurrent reads from q_live identity and market-publication tables, SEC XBRL facts, FINRA short volume, persisted IBKR borrow, and QMD completed daily bars. The responsive Stock Facts surface provides six high-level facts, detailed short and borrow evidence, dated SEC fundamentals, issuer and listing context, provider taxonomy, safe external company links, identifiers, corporate actions, explicit data notes, and table-level provenance. task: Build deterministic news phrase and causal reaction reference tables
description: Extract one canonical phrase-presence fact per article without retaining repetition counts, label causal post-news price reactions at fixed and session-boundary horizons, and aggregate 2019-2025 phrase probabilities while keeping 2026 held out.
progress: Retained the 81-concept and 277-variant language dictionary, article-level phrase presence, XNYS-aware horizons, auditable quality evidence, restart checkpoints, and 2019-2025 statistics split. Replaced the incorrect fixed-clock one-second-bar authority with exact canonical compact events: anchors are the last eligible trade strictly before publication, and terminal/high/low observations use every exact eligible event inside each news-relative interval. The query applies the same condition-token update-last, update-high-low, and extended-hours Form-T rules as QMD. Removed resolution fields and the intraday-bar prerequisite, aligned SPY adjustments to asset-extrema timestamps, versioned physical v2 outputs, and documented the event-relative contract. Publication months build shared exact-event caches from deterministic ticker shards, so each requested ticker's event days and SPY are read once per month. A separate bounded monthly news/ticker cache now materializes canonical publication and availability inputs once; event-cache builders, day workers, and overlap checks reuse it instead of rebuilding multi-million-row joins in every shard. Four bounded day workers reuse both caches, dynamically shard news links at 100 per query with a 64-query ceiling, skip event work for empty days, and preserve completed day checkpoints while rebuilding only incomplete days. Event authority and every chunk are clamped to the configured publication range, so the default build requires only events_2019 through events_2026. Coverage uses active ClickHouse part metadata instead of scanning event payloads. Phrase extraction evaluates each canonical rule once per source field and directly emits its source mask instead of materializing repeatedly expanded global position arrays. Day workers decode only active ticker and exact horizon-date cache rows, retain a continuous SPY benchmark spine, assemble each article/ticker event set once through its maximum horizon, and apply exact horizon caps during metric calculation. Memory-limit failures clear the incomplete day and retry with finer deterministic news shards up to the configured bound.
final_result_or_next_dependency: Sixteen targeted tests and Python compilation pass. The resumed build preserved and advanced 453 completed days and 2,981,900 rows through 2020-03-29 before exposing repeated full-news joins on March 30-31. The exact previously failing 2020-03-31 initial shard now writes 930 rows under the unchanged 6 GiB cap with 2.12 GiB peak memory; selected rows fell from about 10.5 million to 119 thousand and join-build rows from about 10.2 million to 32,898. The 16,210-row bounded news cache matched its canonical source in both EXCEPT directions, and an independent reaction comparison produced 20 rows from each path with zero differences across every persisted field except finalized timestamp. All temporary validation tables were removed and production rows/checkpoints were not mutated by validation. The full 2019-2026 write remains resumable rather than rerun.

progress: Traced the white canvas to lightweight-charts rejecting unresolved semantic CSS variables and traced the lag to the chart waiting on a one-time QMD History preview. The chart resolves semantic theme tokens, retains trustworthy series during interruption, and reconnects with bounded backoff. Recent context loads from the read-only QMD History authority and lazily prepends older covered sessions; current-session updates use QMD Live. Official trade-condition eligibility prevents invalid prints from changing OHLC. The Canvas now enforces its shared point-in-time clock: historical bars whose end exceeds the selected cutoff are withheld, the in-progress QMD current bucket is never merged, and current-session live candles become visible only after their timeframe closes. Historical previews no longer open unnecessary live sockets and identify QMD History honestly. Theme-authoritative session regions shade 04:00-09:30 premarket light orange and 16:00-20:00 after-hours light blue. The history API now uses a lean point-in-time candle projection with exact session/as-of identity and exclusive intra-session cursors, while timeframe changes reuse one bounded Rust cache entry instead of cloning or re-requesting the complete derived payload. Canvas supports the full 100ms, 1s, 5s, 10s, 30s, 1m, 5m, and 1h grid, cancels obsolete requests, stops rerunning unrelated preview work on timeframe changes, and exposes indicators only where the enriched contract actually supplies them. Live 100ms and 5s use filtered canonical trade-family snapshots and revision streams normalized to the same chart candle contract. Corrected the 100ms chart projection so size-only trade-family buckets remain available to the canonical training cache but are removed before live or historical chart limits; subsecond axes now show seconds and tenths, and fixed-grid whitespace uses exact integer-millisecond stepping. Follow-up chart-system correction gives Canvas exclusive scroll ownership through an inner workspace plane; maximized containers fit the Canvas viewport, suppress Canvas and document scrolling, and reserve the right management sidebar. Extended-session shading is clipped to plot geometry rather than axes; crosshair timestamps now match subsecond, second, and minute granularity; the candle current-price line is disabled; and MACD is a default attached oscillator with a sign-colored histogram, bottom-only shared time axis, and persisted legend style and color controls. Every Canvas container now has a visible 22-pixel mouse and keyboard resize target; user pan and zoom survive older-bar prepends; fixed 100ms and 5s indicators use the shared QMD engine; histogram polarity inherits candle colors; extended-session regions cover all plots without covering axes; indicator editors are nearly opaque; attribution and value-guide clutter are removed. Final chart configuration adds a centered loading state, persisted mouse and keyboard oscillator-pane sizing, 180-day daily and 24-month monthly macro views, stable one-shot latest-session and centered-latest controls, and multiple independently persisted chart instances with readable timeframe-derived titles. Same-color chart instances share ticker context while preserving their own timeframe, indicators, pane sizes, appearance, and layout. Repaired the two chart range commands as strictly one-shot actions: subsequent user pan and zoom own the viewport, ordinary streamed updates preserve exact logical coordinates, only true history prepends use time anchoring, and price/oscillator synchronization suppresses echoed programmatic ranges. Replaced the ambiguous calendar-range and target icons with trading-day calendar and horizontal-centering icons, with explicit one-shot accessible labels and tooltips. Macro fit actions now use the loaded timeframe horizon instead of collapsing to one calendar bucket, a separate all-loaded-bars reset is explicit, and every fit writes through the price chart once so pane synchronization cannot echo an older range back after user interaction. Indicator editors render through a viewport-clamped portal with a lightly translucent theme surface, and oscillator series use deterministic histogram-before-line construction so lines remain above bars. The price chart now remains the range authority outside an active oscillator interaction, so delayed pane events cannot reverse Fit session, Center latest, or Reset view. Reset view explicitly restores the persisted candle spacing and right offset, while the indicator editor uses a readable 90 percent theme surface with backdrop blur. The full Indicators and Supervision menus now render through a shared viewport portal instead of inside the clipped chart container, with scale-aware anchor placement, viewport clamping, and the application overlay layer. Indicator style settings now include persisted per-series opacity for lines and histograms. Price is the sole passive horizontal-range authority and synchronizes oscillator ranges plus extended-hours redraws without replaying stale pane events. Grouping preserves each mounted container subtree through stable portal hosts, reserves shared title chrome outside member geometry, retains every chart pane and time axis, and keeps every grouped member stretched through an explicit full-height persistent content chain. The derived splitter capability was removed after browser validation showed nondeterministic resizing and a content-sizing regression. Workspace groups now persist an explicit open or closed lifecycle alongside their hierarchy, geometry, and editable purpose name. Root title bars expose a close action that hides the compound surface without deleting its members or configuration; Manage lists root and nested groups, supports inline rename, identifies open, closed, and nested state, and restores closed groups. Hidden group content is unmounted so closed charts and data containers do not keep invisible subscriptions running. Chart pane layout now treats persisted pane heights as shrinkable preferences, renders each lightweight chart at its actual allocated height, and keeps the bottom time axis inside the pane at the minimum container height. Grouped chart instances now have semantic per-instance render boundaries, local request and viewport state, and instance-keyed appearance storage, so one chart's data, timeframe, indicator, or interaction update cannot redraw an unrelated sibling; explicit color linking continues to share only ticker context. QMD indicator schema v2 separates canonical per-candle trade VWAP from TradingView-compatible cumulative HLC3 VWAP, isolates premarket accumulation, and resets the regular-session benchmark at the exchange's 09:30 New York open.

task: Build database-backed All News, Ticker News, and News Detail containers
description: Replace the generic Canvas news preview with three compact purpose-specific containers: a searchable database table, a symbol-linkable recent feed, and a readable article detail surface.
progress: Added a bounded point-in-time news API with server-side text, ticker, time-window, content, and classification filters plus stable timestamp-and-ID pagination. All News provides a dense searchable table, classification tags, top-level type filtering, and older-page loading. Ticker News uses one product-wide temperature contract: hot through four hours is neon red, cold through 24 hours is neon blue, and old is neutral gray. The versioned deterministic classifier now combines normalized provider tags, channels, author, source links, article language, and ticker scope into explicit kind, origin, format, topic, confidence, evidence, and company-news fields. Classification precedence distinguishes issuer announcements and regulatory filings from analyst actions, generated Insights, AI stories, why-moving coverage, macro releases, multi-company coverage, and ordinary editorial reporting; ticker count is scope evidence only and never makes a single-ticker article company news. Every headline opens one persistent News Reader canvas and reuses its single News Detail container without mutating or closing containers on the originating canvas. News Detail renders the same temperature and classification metadata row as Ticker News together with readable text, provider tags, and source links; per-instance filters and teaser preferences remain persisted. Ticker News now separates newest-first company-specific stories into a priority stream above newest-first analyst, AI, multi-ticker, and market coverage. The news read query now uses the ticker table's ordered ticker/time index only to select matching article identities, derives display tickers from each normalized article, and applies published-date primary-key pruning instead of aggregating every ticker link in the requested window. News content reads have a separate bounded timeout and translate ClickHouse timeout/unavailable states into HTTP 504/503 rather than unhandled ASGI 500 errors. All News now keeps its query-window timestamp on one line, gives date, ET, and VAN time a compact hierarchy, increases ticker and headline readability, and shows the Vancouver date in the Canvas telemetry strip. A separate live contract proxies News Gateway revisions through the backend and refreshes the canonical database query without changing point-in-time Replay, Backtest, or configuration previews. Product news APIs now return explicit presentation DTOs that exclude database/table names, storage paths, raw ingestion diagnostics, and internal implementation fields; Canvas status copy reports only live, reconnecting, or point-in-time freshness. News Detail now identifies explicit monetary prices and rate expressions in headlines and article text without mutating the source, renders prices in semantic blue and percentages/basis points in semantic violet, and adds tabular-number typography so meaning does not depend on color. Years, dates, and ordinary counts remain unaccented; decimal-safe sentence splitting preserves values such as $29.10 and price ranges.
final_result_or_next_dependency: Frontend production build, nine targeted news tests, Python compilation, and diff checks pass. Live ClickHouse-backed queries returned the expected company, multi, analyst, AI, and market classifications. Real-browser validation confirmed source-canvas isolation when opening and closing the reader, reuse of the same reader for a second story, registry visibility in Canvas management, and compact light and dark rendering without horizontal document overflow. The priority-layout follow-up rendered 4 company stories above 16 other stories, verified descending company timestamps, and resolved the dark-theme hot token to neon red #ff3b5c. Exact ml4t reproduction returned the AAPL 72-hour page in 0.102 seconds and the all-news 6-hour page in 0.057 seconds, eliminating the prior 1.8-second timeout path. The latest browser review confirmed All News classification filters and controlled topics, the company-versus-other Ticker News split, News Detail classification evidence, and exact light-theme tokens hot #ff1744, cold #007dff, and old #717182. The exact HCA why-moving article 9b980742dab83b66030b5ac4db529b05 now resolves as editorial why-moving coverage with is_company_news=false in both the SQL list query and Python detail contract, while a verified issuer announcement resolves as company news. A database audit found 204,299 articles carrying bzi-* provider tags, including 180,541 single-ticker articles; the exact b2185e66008f39d6875a8f4449f82b7f article now resolves as Insights in both SQL and detail contracts, renders under Other coverage with bzi-ia visible, and exposes no low-level database or implementation text. The financial-value follow-up passed the production build and strict UI review at light 80%/1600x1000 and dark 125%/1180x760 with zero objective issues. Real-browser checks on HCA and ERAS articles verified intact decimals, ranges, percentages, headline prices, and theme-correct semantic colors.

QMD support, QMD resistance, session, premarket, opening range, swings, BoS, CHoCH, LULD, 52-week, prior-month, POC, and round-price groups now have independent persisted visibility and presentation configuration. The blank-page follow-up fixes the remaining compositor path: range changes reuse one persistent overlay bitmap, clip every canvas and DOM span to the visible plot, group settings once per frame, and constrain navigation to loaded data edges so panning cannot submit enormous off-screen primitives or leave an empty chart. The final blank-page correction fixes the renderer contract itself: backend and frontend ordering now compare parsed RFC3339 instants rather than serialized timestamp text, preventing whole-second values from sorting after fractional-second values on dense subsecond charts. Total retained rows are bounded according to projected indicator width, and every ChartPanel is isolated by a retryable renderer error boundary so a future chart fault cannot unmount the application. The definitive interaction fix now makes strict chronological output a QMD History cache invariant, rejects duplicate or descending derived bars before they can be cached or streamed, prevents synchronous visible-range propagation from re-entering sibling panes, validates every propagated logical range, coalesces independent live bar and indicator sockets into one animation-frame update, and evicts live rows in bounded chunks so a full multi-series setData is no longer forced on every 100 ms tick after capacity. The repeated-interaction root-cause correction replaces callback-to-callback pane range writes with one animation-frame coordinator: only the pane owning the user gesture may publish, programmatic peer callbacks can never become authoritative, and normalized peer ranges are never written back from inside a subscription callback. A shared post-gesture numerical guard also compares each rendered price transform with cached loaded-data bounds and returns only non-finite, collapsed, or more-than-10,000-times pathological manual scales to autoscale before canvas coordinates overflow. The fundamental renderer follow-up removes the multi-chart topology entirely: Lightweight Charts 5.2 now owns candles, volume, and every oscillator as native panes in one chart with one time scale, one crosshair, and one interaction lifecycle. Price and oscillator legends, session overlays, persisted pane heights, thresholds, and close/configure controls are positioned over the native pane elements without range or crosshair mirroring. The pane-layout and presentation follow-up removes the competing custom resize layer and makes Lightweight Charts native stretch factors the single persisted authority. A pane-element ResizeObserver keeps each HTML legend attached to its owning pane during drag, including the price pane. QMD structure now defaults to decision and tactical zones, opening range, and POC; hides lower-priority groups until requested; clips active evidence to recent spans; removes stale controls; and uses confidence-weighted sign color. The price-overlay canvas excludes candle bodies and wicks so every zone, connector, and annotation remains visually behind candles. The current-edge structure contract now exposes a bounded canonical active-level candidate set from QMD rather than only one winner per scale: up to eight nearest active supports and resistances plus the strongest candidate when it is farther away. Canvas ranks that latest causal snapshot by distance, displays a configurable one to six nearest zones per side with a default of three, always includes the strongest support and resistance when outside that set, marks strongest additions with an asterisk, and encodes confidence through borderless fill intensity plus an internal percentage label. Historical segments now split whenever the causal confidence or strength decile changes, so later evidence cannot repaint an earlier segment. The role-correction follow-up advances the structure algorithm to version 3, indicator schema to 14, and historical cache authority to qmd-derived-v14. Every zone now follows an explicit persisted lifecycle: active, boundary-breach candidate, awaiting retest, retest contact, rejection candidate, or retired. A zone containing current price is omitted as in play; exposed support requires the full zone below reference and exposed resistance requires the full zone above reference. A scale-specific break needs midpoint persistence plus an eligible trade beyond the far boundary, retires the original role, and emits a durable level_break event. The opposite role is created only after a later retest from the broken side and a separately confirmed rejection, emits a durable role_reversal event, and starts with fresh evidence rather than inherited confidence. Event replay retires the original lineage before restoring the flipped side, preventing duplicate future reversals. The chart-artifact follow-up separates conservative candle label-collision bounds from compositor geometry: price-zone overlays now exclude only the exact open-to-close body and narrow high-to-low wick, eliminating the tall background-colored holes previously cut through dense support and resistance regions. The one-minute regression proved that any destructive transparency on the foreground overlay still exposed chart background for wide or tall candles, so that mask-based correction is superseded. QMD zone fills and structure connectors now render as a native Lightweight Charts series primitive at bottom z-order; the chart library paints candles afterward, while the existing overlay canvas is limited to collision-managed text labels and never erases chart pixels.

task: Prevent background Canvas container failures from blanking the workspace
description: Reproduce the persistent blank-page failure using the user's real 10-second Canvas state, trace it across the active frontend and backend lifecycle, and fix the actual source without another chart-specific workaround.
progress: Copied the user's Chrome localStorage into an isolated Playwright profile and reproduced the full application disappearing on interaction 12 with the exact AAPL 10-second fullscreen chart and background Canvas containers. The captured page exception identified the source: the SEC list service returned scalar item values for four rows, while the SEC table treated every value as a string array and called join; because background containers remained mounted, the uncaught late render exception unmounted the entire workspace during otherwise unrelated chart interaction. The SEC backend now enforces list-valued items before classification and response serialization, while the frontend normalizes array, scalar, JSON-array, comma-delimited, null, and unknown wire values at its API boundary. Every Canvas container portal is also isolated by a retryable error boundary so any future container-specific rendering defect remains local instead of destroying the workspace. The browser review harness now captures renderer crashes and page errors, supports exact indicator seeding and 10-second focus stress, reports bounded render state during interaction, and can force left-edge history paging. The right-edge follow-up removes the contradictory fixed-right-edge constraint from the shared native time scale while keeping the historical left edge bounded. Traders can now drag the latest bar left to reserve future space, and the explicit new-bar shift policy preserves that working offset as live bars arrive. The browser harness now asserts the future-space gesture in both Canvas and chart-focus routes.
final_result_or_next_dependency: The SEC unit suite passes, the frontend TypeScript/Vite production build passes, and the live SEC endpoint returned 100 of 100 rows with array-valued items after backend restart. task: Train embedding-only single-ticker news reaction model v1
description: Create a fast first-phase production model that consumes only frozen Qwen embeddings for genuinely single-ticker news and forecasts ticker price reaction across all agreed intraday and session-close horizons, using an exact news-ID, ticker, and publication-time match to certified reaction labels and a strict 2019-2025 train versus 2026 evaluation split.
progress: Added an isolated news_reaction_model/v1 package with a versioned resumable monthly ClickHouse preparation stage, bounded concurrent keyset loader, masked two-chunk embedding pooler, horizon-conditioned residual shared encoder, three-class and abnormal target/high/low heads, joint loss, calibration and per-horizon metrics, sample-clock training, asynchronous checkpoints, W&B and redacted manifests, model artifacts and cards, production inference, diagram and metric notebooks, and a model-size plus batch-size throughput profiler. The network input is strictly the frozen article embedding and chunk-presence mask; publication session remains label-construction metadata and is not a model feature. The expensive exact source join is materialized once; training reads only the prepared versioned table. Exact range manifests prevent partial rows from being mistaken for completed months. Terminal output keeps durable JSONL detail while presenting compact progress, ETA, throughput, failures, and completion state. Masked chunk pooling uses negative infinity rather than the finite dtype minimum so bf16 AMP cannot overflow during scalar conversion; profiler failures are retained per configuration instead of aborting without a summary. The default 96 GB GPU sweep now measures batches from 512 through 32,768 against the full prepared 2019-2026 range. Its source sampler assembles the requested article count across the training loader's bounded monthly batches and rejects a genuinely undersized range instead of silently profiling a truncated batch. Training compilation follows the proven temporal-v3 capability contract: CUDA without Triton is reported and runs eager PyTorch with otherwise identical semantics instead of failing lazily on the first batch. Resume keeps PyTorch's restricted weights-only loading, narrowly allowlists legacy WindowsPath metadata, and writes future checkpoint configs with string paths.
final_result_or_next_dependency: Ten focused unit tests and Python compilation pass, including explicit bfloat16 autocast, profiler source-capacity, cross-month exact-sample, missing-Triton compilation, and legacy secure-resume regressions. A bounded real ClickHouse build produced 6,888 exactly matched January 2026 articles in 10.5 seconds with zero invalid chunks, targets, split leakage, or duplicate article identities; the user completed the full preparation with 486,078 exact articles and no failed months or invalid rows. A live preflight assembled exactly 32,768 distinct-shaped profile rows across monthly batches with the expected two-by-1,024 embedding contract. The full workstation profiler completed 84 of 84 configurations without OOM; the throughput maximum was d128-l1-b32768, while d384-l4-b2048 was selected as the capacity-aware training candidate. The completed d384-l4-b2048 run trained three epochs over 441,793 articles per epoch, wrote 1,325,379-sample latest/best/archive checkpoints, and beat uniform 2026 holdout log loss by 0.0285. The first 50-epoch resume attempt was blocked before training because PyTorch 2.6 restricted loading rejected legacy WindowsPath metadata; the corrected loader read the actual 21 MB checkpoint and restored epoch 3, sample 1,325,379, optimizer, and scheduler state without unrestricted pickle. Forty source and shared MLOps files were synced to the workstation runtime with zero SHA-256 mismatches and no copied .env; the runtime is refreshed after each correction.
